title: ficus jacquelineae
intro: Ficus jacquelineae, also known as the Jacqueline's fig, is a species of fig tree native to New Guinea. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Ficus jacquelineae include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health. Herbal preparations of Ficus jacquelineae can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or by creating a tincture to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
